properties.description: The SVG path describes a zigzag shape characteristic of a lightning bolt, pointing downward with a step-like structure suggesting power or energy

properties.usage: main fill of lightning bolt shape

properties.description: Cyan blue appears as accent to contrast the dominant purple palette

properties.technique: feGaussianBlur SVG filters applied to masked ellipses creating internal luminosity

properties.description: Favicon serves as brand identity icon for the TradeCatalysts application
Primary and dark purple form a coherent monochromatic brand palette
Lightning bolt commonly symbolizes speed, energy, power — fitting for a trading/catalyst platform name

links.note: File is stored in the FE/src/assets directory, making it a frontend static asset

Image shows two stacked rounded-rectangle card shapes in isometric perspective — top card is outlined/transparent, bottom card has a vivid purple gradient edge, suggesting a layered platform or data-stack concept
The illustration is rendered in a clean isometric 3-D perspective with minimal detail and a white/transparent background
The bottom card features a vivid purple-to-violet gradient on its side faces, establishing a brand accent color
The layered card motif relies on the purple accent to create visual hierarchy between the two layers
assets directory is a sub-directory of the FE project
Frontend assets directory in a React project contains the React logo, indicating the frontend is built with React
SVG title element explicitly says 'Vite'; logo uses Vite brand colors (purple #9135ff, violet #8900ff, cyan #00c2ff) and the recognisable lightning-bolt-in-diamond shape
Vite logo is a default template asset used as the project favicon/logo in Vite-scaffolded apps
Presence of the Vite logo in FE/src/assets strongly indicates the frontend project is scaffolded with Vite (standard Vite template includes this asset)
SVG file is the official React logo with cyan/blue atomic orbital design, aria-label class iconify--logos, visually depicting the React brand
SVG has class iconify iconify--logos indicating it comes from the Iconify icon library logos set
